author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXVIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …We are taught in this Psalm, when we chaunt Allelujah, which meaneth, Praise the Lord, that we should, when we hear the words, “Confess unto the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1), praise the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The praise of God could not be expressed in fewer words than these, “For He is good.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I see not what can be more solemn than this brevity, since goodness is so peculiarly the quality of God, that the Son of God Himself when addressed by some one as “Good Master,” by one, namely, who beholding His flesh, and comprehending not the fulness of His divine nature, considered Him as man only, replied, “Why callest thou Me good?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is none good but one, that is, God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is this but to say, If thou wishest to call Me good, recognise Me as God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since it is addressed, in revelation of things to come, to a people freed from all toil and wandering in pilgrimage, and from all admixture with the wicked, which freedom was given it through the grace of God, who not only doth not evil for evil, but even returneth good for evil; it is most appropriately added, “Because His mercy endureth for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let Israel now confess that He is good, and that His mercy endureth for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the house of Aaron now confess that His mercy endureth for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Yea, let all now that fear the Lord confess that His mercy endureth for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye remember, I suppose, most beloved, what is the house of Israel, what is the house of Aaron, and that both are those that fear the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they are “the little and the great,” who have already in another Psalm been happily introduced into your hearts: in the number of whom all of us should rejoice that we are joined together, in His grace who is good, and whose mercy endureth for ever; since they were listened to who said, “May the Lord increase you more and more, you and your children;” that the host of the Gentiles might be added to the Israelites who believed in Christ, of the number of whom are the Apostles our fathers, for the exaltation of the perfect and the obedience of the little children; that all of us when made one in Christ, made one flock under one Shepherd, and the body of that Head, like one man, may say, “I called upon the Lord in trouble, and the Lord heard me at large” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The narrow straits of our tribulation are limited: but the large way whereby we pass along hath no end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who shall lay anything to the charge of God’s elect?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is my helper; I will not fear what man doeth unto me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But are men, then, the only enemies that the Church hath?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is a man devoted to flesh and blood, save flesh and blood?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Apostle saith, “We wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against,”…he saith, “spiritual wickedness in high places;” that is, the devil and his angels; that devil whom elsewhere he calleth “the prince of the power of the air.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear therefore what followeth: “The Lord is my helper: therefore shall I despise mine enemies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From what class soever my enemies may arise, whether from the number of evil men, or from the number of evil angels; in the Lord’s help, unto whom we chant the confession of praise, unto whom we sing Allelujah, they shall be despised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, when my enemies have been brought to contempt, let not my friend present himself unto me as a good man, so as to bid me repose my hope in himself: for “It is better to trust in the Lord, than to put any confidence in man” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor let any one, who may in a certain sense be styled a good angel, be regarded by myself as one in whom I ought to put my trust: for “no one is good, save God alone;” and when a man or an angel appear to aid us, when they do this of sincere affection, He doth it through them, who made them good after their measure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It is” therefore “better to trust in the Lord, than to put any confidence in princes” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For angels also are called princes, even as we read in Daniel, “Michael, your prince.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All nations compassed me round about, but in the Name of the Lord have I taken vengeance on them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They kept me in on every side, they kept me in, I say, on every side; but in the Name of the Lord have I taken vengeance on them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He signifieth the toils and the victory of the Church; but, as if the question were asked how she could have overcome so great evils, he looketh back to the example, and declareth what she had first suffered in her Head, by adding what followeth, “They kept me in on every side:” and the words, “All nations,” are with reason not repeated here, because this was the act of the Jews alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There that very religious nation (which is the body of Christ, and in behalf of which was done all that was done in mortal form with immortal power, by that inward divinity, through the outward flesh), suffered from persecutors, of whose race that flesh was assumed and hung upon the cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They came about me as bees do a hive, and burned up even as the fire among the thorns: and in the Name of the Lord have I taken vengeance on them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here then the order of the words corresponds with the order of events.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we rightly understand that our Lord Himself, the Head of the Church, was surrounded by persecutors, even as bees surround a hive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Holy Spirit is speaking with mystic subtlety of what was done by those who knew not what they did.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For bees make honey in the hives: while our Lord’s persecutors, unconscious as they were, rendered Him sweeter unto us even by His very Passion; so that we may taste and see how sweet is the Lord, “Who died for our sins, and arose for our justification.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what followeth, “and burned up even as the fire among the thorns,” is better understood of His Body, that is, of a people spread abroad, whom all nations compassed about, since it was gathered together from all nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They consumed this sinful flesh, and the grievous piercings of this mortal life, in the flame of persecution.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Taken vengeance on them:” either because they themselves, that wickedness, which in them persecuted the righteous, having been extinguished, were joined with the people of Christ; or because the rest of them, who have at this time scorned the mercy of Him who calleth them, will at the end feel the truth of Him who judgeth them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have been driven on like a heap of sand, so that I was falling, but the Lord upheld me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For though there were a great multitude of believers, that might be compared to the countless sand, and brought into one communion as into one heap; yet “what is man, save Thou be mindful of Him?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not, the multitude of the Gentiles could not surpass the abundance of my host, but, “the Lord,” he saith, “hath upheld me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The persecution of the Gentiles succeeded not in pushing forward, to its overthrow, the host of the faithful dwelling together in the unity of the faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is my strength and my praise, and is become my salvation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who then fall, when they are pushed, save they who choose to be their own strength and their own praise?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For no man falleth in the contest, except he whose strength and praise faileth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore whose strength and praise is the Lord, falleth no more than the Lord falleth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And for this reason He hath become their salvation; not that He hath become anything which He was not before, but because they, when they believed on Him, became what they were not before, and then He began to be salvation unto them when turned towards Him, which He was not to them when turned away from Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The voice of joy and health is in the dwellings of the righteous” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 15); where they who raged against their bodies thought there was the voice of sorrow and destruction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they did not know the inward joy of the saints in their future hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence the Apostle also saith, “As sorrowful, yet alway rejoicing;” and again, “And not only so, but we glory in tribulations also.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The right hand of the Lord hath brought mighty things to pass” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What mighty things? saith he.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The right hand of the Lord,” he saith, “hath exalted me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a mighty thing to exalt the humble, to deify the mortal, to bring perfection out of infirmity, glory from subjection, victory from suffering, to give help, to raise from trouble; that the true salvation of God might be laid open to the afflicted, and the salvation of men might remain of no avail to the persecutors.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are great things: but what art thou surprised at? hear what he repeateth: “The right hand of the Lord hath brought mighty things to pass.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I shall not die, but live, and declare the works of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they, while they were dealing havoc and death on every side, thought that the Church of Christ was dying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, he now declareth the works of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Everywhere Christ is the glory of the blessed Martyrs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By being beaten He conquered those who struck Him; by being patient of torments, the tormentors; by loving, those who raged against Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, let him point out to us, why the body of Christ, the holy Church, the people of adoption, suffered such indignities.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord,” he saith, “hast chastened and corrected me, but He hath not given me over unto death” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not then the boastful wicked imagine that aught hath been permitted to their power: they would not have that power, were it not given them from above.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Oft doth the father of a family command his sons to be corrected by the most worthless slaves; though he designeth the heritage for the former, fetters for the latter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that heritage?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it of gold, or silver, or jewels, or farms, or pleasant estates?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider how we enter into it: and learn what it is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Open me,” he saith, “the gates of righteousness” (ver 19).

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, we have heard of the gates.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is within?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That I may,” he saith, “go into them, and give thanks unto the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the confession of praise full of wonder, “even unto the house of God, in the voice of joy and confession of praise, among such as keep holiday:” this is the everlasting bliss of the righteous, whereby they are blessed who dwell in the Lord’s house, praising Him for evermore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But consider how the gates of righteousness are entered into.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “These are the gates of the Lord” he saith, “the righteous shall enter into them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At least let no wicked man enter there, that Jerusalem which receiveth not one uncircumcised, where it is said, “Without are dogs.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be it enough, that in my long pilgrimage “I have had my habitation among the tents of Kedar:” I endured even unto the end the intercourse of the wicked, but “these are the gates of the Lord: the righteous shall enter into them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will confess unto Thee, O Lord, for Thou hast heard me, and art become my salvation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How often is that confession proved to be one of praise, that doth not point out wounds to the physician, but giveth thanks for the health it hath received.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Physician Himself is the Salvation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who is this whom we speak of?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Stone which the builders rejected” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 22); for “It hath become the head Stone of the corner” to “make in Himself of twain one new man, so making peace; and that He might reconcile both unto God in one body;” circumcision, to wit, and uncircumcision.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “By the Lord was it made unto it” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 23): that is, it is made into the head stone of the corner by the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For although He would not have become this, had He not suffered: yet He became not this through those from whom He suffered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they who were building, refused Him: but in the edifice which the Lord was secretly raising, that was made the head stone of the corner which they rejected.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And it is marvellous in our eyes:” in the eyes of the inner man, in the eyes of those that believe, those that hope, those that love; not in the carnal eyes of those who, through scorning Him as if He were a man, rejected Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This is the day which the Lord hath made” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This man remembereth that he had said in former Psalms, “Since He hath inclined His ear unto me, therefore will I call upon Him as long as I live;” making mention of his old days; whence he now saith, “This is the day which the Lord hath made;” that is, wherein He hath given me Salvation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the day whereof He said, “In an acceptable time have I heard thee, and in a day of Salvation have I helped thee;” that is, a day wherein He, the Mediator, hath become the head Stone of the corner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let us rejoice,” therefore, “and be glad in Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Save me now, O Lord: prosper Thou well my way, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it is the day of Salvation, “save me:” because we, returning from a long pilgrimage, are separated from those who hated peace, with whom we were peaceful, and who, when we spoke to them, made war upon us without a cause; “prosper well our way” as we return, since Thou hast become our Way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed be He that cometh in the Name of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Cursed, therefore, is he that cometh in his own name; as He saith in the Gospel: “if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We have blessed you out of the house of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I believe that these are the words of the great to the little, of those great ones, to wit, who in spirit commune with God the Word, who is with God, as they may in this life; and yet temper their discourse for the sake of the little ones, so that they may sincerely say what the Apostle saith: “For whether we be beside ourselves, it is to God: or whether we be sober, it is for your cause.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the love of Christ constraineth us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They bless the little children from the inner house of the Lord, where that praise faileth not age after age: consider therefore what they proclaim from thence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God is the Lord, who hath showed us light” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Lord, who came in the Lord’s Name, whom the builders refused, and who became the head Stone of the corner, that “Mediator between God and man, Jesus Christ,” is God, He is equal with the Father, He hath showed us light, that we might understand what we believed, and declare it to you who understand it not as yet, but already believe it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that ye also may understand, “Declare a holy day in full assemblies, even unto the horns of the altar;” that is, even unto the inner house of God, from which we have blessed you, where are the high places of the altar.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Declare a holy day,” not in a slothful manner, but “in full assemblies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this is the voice of joyfulness among those that keep holy day, who walk “in the place of the wonderful tabernacle, even unto the house of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if there be there the spiritual sacrifice, the everlasting sacrifice of praise, both the Priest is everlasting, and the peaceful mind of the righteous an everlasting altar.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what shall we sing there, save His praises?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What else shall we say there, save, “Thou art my God, and I will confess unto Thee; Thou art my God, and I will praise Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I wilt confess unto Thee, for Thou hast heard me, and art become my Salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We will not say these things in loud words; but the love that abideth in Him of itself crieth out in these words, and these words are love itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus as he began with praise, so he endeth: “Confess unto the Lord, for He is gracious, and His mercy endureth for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With this the Psalm commenceth, with this it endeth; since, as from the commencement which we have left behind, so in the end, whither we are returning, there is not anything that can more profitably please us, than the praise of God, and Allelujah evermore.
